mnemonics: CIA Triad: Confidentiality (secrecy), Integrity (trustworthiness), Availability (accessibility) - Remember it as 'Can I Access' my data securely and correctly?
keyPoints: The CIA Triad (Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability) is the cornerstone of data security.
Cryptography is a fundamental tool for ensuring confidentiality and integrity.
Access control mechanisms enforce policies on who can access or modify data and resources.
commonMistakes: Confusing privacy (a policy-driven concept about personal data) with security (the technical mechanisms to protect all data).
Focusing only on confidentiality (encryption) and neglecting the equally important principles of integrity and availability.
